what_it_does: This file is a ledger attachment record that registers an image asset in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to a target image URI and a creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
standouts: Target URI contains a literal space and parentheses: image://design-assets/icons/image (31)
It is an image attachment in the design-assets channel
It has a precise ISO 8601 added timestamp
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable att-* id
Uses an image:// URI scheme to reference the asset
Records an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
File path places it under channel-memory/channel_attachments/
concerns: Target URI contains an unescaped space and parentheses, which may be fragile for parsers or link resolution
No display name, alt text, dimensions, format, or checksum metadata is provided
The meaning of 'image (31)' is opaque from this file alone
notes: A minimal, timestamped ledger stub that points to a design-assets image via a URI with an unusual space-and-parentheses name.
questions: What does 'image (31)' refer to and where is the actual file stored?
Is the target URI intended to be resolvable locally, remotely, or only as a stable reference?
What role or context does this attachment serve in the design-assets channel?
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a durable, id-addressable record of an image attachment so the design-assets channel can reference it by id and timestamp.
novelty: The target URI embeds a space and parentheses rather than a normalized slug, which is uncommon for machine-readable references.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, id-stamped reference to a specific image attachment in the design-assets channel, supporting lookup and provenance without requiring the asset itself to be embedded.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a ledger attachment record for the design-assets channel that registers an image attachment with id att-e0afd2d6 pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (31) and a 2026-06-22 creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to catalog and make addressable a specific image asset within the design-assets channel.
